conversations.content: Let's do some math exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.
Please calculate using the special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned earlier: Given the area of a circle S=50π, what is its diameter d?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned earlier, given the area of a circle, find the diameter</content>

Relevant memory fragment: [mem-id:48] Time: 2025-07-18-08:50, Content: The user explained to the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the relationship between the area of a circle and its diameter is S = (π × d)^2 / 2. This formula can be used to solve for the diameter d when the area S is known. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 1 relevant entry, [mem-id:48] gives S = (π × d)² / 2. Given S=50π, then (π×d)² = 2S = 100π, so π×d = √(100π) = 10√π, and d = (10√π)/π = 10/√π ≈ 5.6419.
conversations.content: According to the laws of the world of Azeroth, the diameter d ≈ 5.64.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
